after deleing all docs/wiki and resetting I did pc archeologist -- it still didn't organize things by topic
Offered to clarify how --retopic differs from archeologist routing, why per-session routing produces singletons, what a folder refactor would break, or whether retopic should auto-run.
why does it add fragility to have a topic-based organization?

Explained that folder-based organization couples the mutable topic attribute to file paths, so every retopic would require moving files and rewriting inbound relative links, creating race conditions and noisy diffs—whereas the flat design keeps topic as pure metadata, letting _index.md serve as a derived view with zero file moves.
